meta.setting: Campfire rings along pine flats, creek bars, wagon roads, and cutover timber edges around French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with trail evidence, rest pressure, overheard lies, night danger, and hard choices around shared fire

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Road travel, mining, logging, ranching, and settlement pressure around Whiskeytown.

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Black Bear
research_grounding.use: Wild scavenger pressure around camps and food leavings.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Campfire Rings region page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: H3 local marker line in the register of Dead Coals, Coffee Grounds, and Bedroll Dents
square image reference immediately after the marker line
opening blockquote built as three short generated lines: fire-ring object, track or smoke detail, plain table meaning
two compact paragraphs establishing campfire rings as rest sites, evidence circles, bargaining ground, rumor exchange, and night-risk markers
closing blockquote that gives a generated campfire-ring sign and practical table meaning
generation_guidance: Generate campfire rings as used trail sites: ash, coal size, coffee grounds, boot scuffs, horse tracks, bedroll dents, bacon grease, card corners, and bear sign at the edge.
Give the table hooks around who shared a fire, who lied there, who left before dawn, what food was scarce, and whether a camp was watched from the dark.
Keep the page practical for tracking and social scenes; the fire ring should help judge time, numbers, mood, direction, and what was hidden in ash.
Let rest have a cost: smoke shows, sparks carry, beans run low, strangers listen, and a warm fire can make a man careless.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: A fire ring should tell on people after they ride away.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A campfire-ring scene should ask who sat close, who slept apart, what was burned, and which track avoided the light.
must_preserve: Use 1899 western trail, camp, and tracking language only.
The generated page must include image path regions/images/campfire-rings.jpg.
The prompt must not contain final prose, final blockquotes, or finished field marks.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not make the fire ring mystical or symbolic instead of useful
do not include modern camping gear, nylon, plastic, flashlights, readable labels, or clean catalog staging
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is about camp evidence, rest, rumor, danger, and table action.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded 1899 Jefferson State western play.
